title: Download OSM Traces
text: goal osmtraces help downloading publicly available traces OpenStreetMaps

title: Download OSM Traces
text: can install development version osmtraces like :

title: Download OSM Traces
text: basic example shows solve common problem:

what: Download all public traces from a geographic location — download_osm_tracks
title: Download all public traces from a geographic location — download_osm_tracks
text: publicly available traces traversing bounding box specified lat, lon zoom downloaded OpenStreetMaps. Downloads paginated; page contains maximum 5000 track points saved file folder/page-X.gpx, folder either user-specified temporary folder. message informs user case download complete.

title: Download all public traces from a geographic location — download_osm_tracks
code: download_osm_tracks(lon, lat, zoom = 16, first_page = 0, folder = NULL)

title: Download all public traces from a geographic location — download_osm_tracks
text: lon geographic longitude lat geographic latitude zoom value specifying zoom value (bounding box centered around specified latitude longitude) first_page integer value indicating page number start scrape; API allows download 5000 points page. default 20 pages downloaded. case points exist, re-run download increased values first_page = 20, 40, ... folder character string destination folder. existing files overwritten.

title: Download all public traces from a geographic location — download_osm_tracks
text: character string download folder.

title: Download all public traces from a geographic location — download_osm_tracks
code: # The website # https://www.openstreetmap.org/#map=15/42.00709/-93.57195&layers=PG # translates to if (interactive()) {   download_osm_tracks( -93.57195, 42.00709, 15, first_page = 0,                        folder = "ames")   download_osm_tracks( -93.57195, 42.00709, 15, first_page = 20,                        folder = "ames")   download_osm_tracks( -93.57195, 42.00709, 15, first_page = 40,                        folder = "ames") }

what: Derive heading based on consecutive geographic locations — gps_heading
title: Derive heading based on consecutive geographic locations — gps_heading
text: heading device gps unit can determined calculating angle last gps location current location. assumed lon lat sorted temporally.

title: Derive heading based on consecutive geographic locations — gps_heading
Derive heading based on consecutive geographic locations — gps_heading
text: lon vector geographic longitudes lat vector geographic latitudes type character value describing type angle value - either "degree" (default) "radians"

title: Derive heading based on consecutive geographic locations — gps_heading
text: vector gps heading type input.

what: Derive speed based on geographic location — gps_speed_mph
title: Derive speed based on geographic location — gps_speed_mph
text: Time distance consecutive geographic locations used derive speed trace traversed.

title: Derive speed based on geographic location — gps_speed_mph
Derive speed based on geographic location — gps_speed_mph
text: lon vector geographic longitudes lat vector geographic latitudes time vector time stamps

title: Derive speed based on geographic location — gps_speed_mph
text: vector speed miles per hour (mph)

what: Read OSM traces from a gpx file — read_gpx
title: Read OSM traces from a gpx file — read_gpx
text: OpenStreetMap API returns traces form paged gpx (v1.0) files. function used stand-alone gpx reader - meant read downloaded trace files.

title: Read OSM traces from a gpx file — read_gpx
Read OSM traces from a gpx file — read_gpx
text: file file path gpx file

title: Read OSM traces from a gpx file — read_gpx
text: data frame traces

title: Read OSM traces from a gpx file — read_gpx
code: # example code if (interactive()) { library(ggplot2) read_gpx(system.file("ames/page-0.gpx", package = "osmtraces")) |>   tidyr::unnest(trkseg) |>   ggplot(aes(x = lon, y = lat)) + geom_point() }

what: Read pre-downloaded traces from a folder — read_osm_traces
title: Read pre-downloaded traces from a folder — read_osm_traces
text: Read pre-downloaded traces folder

title: Read pre-downloaded traces from a folder — read_osm_traces
Read pre-downloaded traces from a folder — read_osm_traces
text: folder character vector file path gpx files located

title: Read pre-downloaded traces from a folder — read_osm_traces
text: data frame one row trace; variables usually include file name, description, url. Traces included list variable data frames. trace sequence time stamped geographic locations.

title: Read pre-downloaded traces from a folder — read_osm_traces
code: # example code if (interactive()) { library(ggplot2) folder <- system.file("ames", package = "osmtraces") ames <- read_osm_traces(folder) ames$data[[1]] |> ggplot(aes(x = lon, y = lat)) + geom_point()   download_osm_tracks(lon = -96.707931, lat = 40.811909, zoom = 16,  folder="lincoln") lincoln_tracks <- read_osm_traces("lincoln")  lincoln_tracks <- lincoln_tracks |> dplyr::mutate(   data = data |> purrr::map(.f = function(df) {     df$gps_heading = gps_heading(lon=df$lon, lat=df$lat)     df$gps_speed = gps_speed_mph(lon=df$lon, lat=df$lat, df$time)     df     })  )  library(dplyr) lincoln_trackpoints <- lincoln_tracks |>   mutate(rowid = 1:n()) |> tidyr::unnest(data)  lincoln_trackpoints |> ggplot(aes(x = lon, y = lat, colour = gps_heading)) +   geom_path(aes(group=rowid)) + scale_colour_gradientn(     colours = grDevices::hcl(       h = seq(0, 360, length.out = 361),       c = 100,       l = 65     ),     limits = c(0, 360),     breaks = seq(0, 360, by = 45)   ) }

what: Turn a track into multiple segments — segmentize
title: Turn a track into multiple segments — segmentize
text: time successive time points exceeds interval specified seconds, assumed hat new segment started.

title: Turn a track into multiple segments — segmentize
Turn a track into multiple segments — segmentize
text: time vector time stamps seconds positive value indicating time allowed successive time points new segment started.

title: Turn a track into multiple segments — segmentize
text: integer vector segment ids

what: Convert a trace into a data frame — trace_to_df
title: Convert a trace into a data frame — trace_to_df
text: Turns GPX defined track format xml node data frame. elements turned variables, track single-entry list variable data frame time-stamped geographic locations. function called part read_gpx. need call function .

title: Convert a trace into a data frame — trace_to_df
Convert a trace into a data frame — trace_to_df
text: trk xml node elements attributes

title: Convert a trace into a data frame — trace_to_df
text: single row dataframe nested dataframe trace form tibble variables latitude, longitude, time.
